thesis: Q1 2026 earnings beat: 32% YoY revenue growth, first $1B+ quarterly revenue. FedRAMP High certification opens federal market. AI-native customer demand accelerating — included in 'forgotten AI trade' rebound alongside NOW and CRM. Oppenheimer PT $200, Guggenheim $175. Software ETFs (IGV/WCLD) outpacing QQQ.

Rezdiffra blockbuster (>$1.1B trailing 12M net sales, first-in-disease MASH therapy). Q1 2026: $311M net sales, +127% YoY. 42,250+ patients on drug, growing 2.5x YoY. April 2026 strongest month for new prescriptions. Avg analyst PT $671 = +33% upside. Strong Buy consensus from 13 analysts. R&D pipeline expanding with siRNA combination regimens.

Q1 EPS $1.06 vs $0.60 consensus — massive beat. Strait of Hormuz closed since Feb 28 = structural oil price tailwind (Brent $104-111). Berkshire Hathaway (Greg Abel) maintained OXY position in Q1 2026. $7.1B debt repaid. Raymond James PT $75. Up 30% YTD. Risk: Iran peace deal could pressure oil prices.

Q2 FY2026: +17% revenue, +20% adjusted EPS, cross-border volume +12%. $20B buyback authorized. Berkshire maintained position. Trump fintech EO positive for payment processors. Durable network-effect moat. Consumer spending resilient. Whale options activity signals institutional conviction.

Defensive retail leader in high-inflation, low-consumer-confidence environment. Consumer flight to value. Q1 earnings May 21: consensus +5.4% revenue, +7.9% EPS. Up 17% YTD. Analyst PTs $136-$140. AI/tech workforce restructuring targets margin improvement. Trades at premium but justified by defensive positioning.
